schema_note: Z-LEGAL-BENCH-1 internal readiness metadata — operator-edited signals only; not live scoring from user behaviour; not legal advice or certification.
disclaimer_lines: Not permission to launch or market services as compliant.
Signals are illustrative placeholders for lawyer and operator discussion — AMK/legal review decides next steps.

signal_legend.meaning: Documentation/evidence appears ready for the next governance or legal review step (still not certification).
Human or qualified legal decision required before treating the area as launch-safe.
Needs improvement, clarification, or more evidence before advancing.
Treat as blocking public launch or strong product claims until reviewed.
Future concept or roadmap-only — not a present operational claim.
Internally strong narrative or artifacts — still not certification or legal sign-off.

categories.title: AI governance readiness
categories.measure: AI Act awareness, human-gated automation, 14 DRP posture, provider/API boundaries, prohibited-practice and literacy obligations as applicable — internal checklist only.
categories.notes: Regulatory timelines and scopes evolve; map obligations to your actual stack with counsel.

categories.title: GDPR / data protection readiness
categories.measure: Controller–processor clarity, data-processing agreements where personal data is processed on behalf of a controller, access/deletion posture, sub-processors, records of processing — documentation gap scan only.
categories.notes: Ireland DPC and other regulators publish controller–processor contract guidance; align contracts and facts with counsel.

categories.title: Product / IP readiness
categories.measure: Invention logs, trademark searches/strategy, copyright ownership, patents where applicable, manufacturer and claims boundaries — inventory only.
categories.notes: Product workstation maps are conceptual until IP counsel validates.

categories.title: Commercial / pricing readiness
categories.measure: Pricing clarity, refund terms, entitlement boundaries across projects, no accidental cross-entitlement — policy text and registry alignment only.
categories.notes: Cross-project SKUs and bundles need explicit human-readable boundaries.

categories.title: Accessibility readiness
categories.measure: WCAG / EAA / EN 301 549-style checklist posture — readiness indicators, not a certification badge.
categories.notes: Use audits and tooling; do not label as certified without an accredited process.

categories.title: Deployment readiness (e.g. Cloudflare Pages)
categories.measure: Git integration vs Direct Upload vs C3 posture, domain, privacy policy and terms presence, rollback plan, staging vs public — launch checklist only.
categories.notes: Pages can deploy via Git, Direct Upload, or C3; pair technical go-live with legal/compliance review before public marketing.

categories.title: Evidence / audit readiness
categories.measure: Receipts, reports, green/blue/red signals, execution enforcer and registry verify outputs — traceability for humans.
categories.notes: Strong internal discipline does not replace external legal sign-off.

categories.title: Risk queue readiness
categories.measure: Items routed for lawyer review before launch — derived from governance reports and human triage, not auto-prioritised.
categories.notes: BLUE in hub reports means decision/review gate; align language with counsel.

risk_queue_banner: Items marked BLUE or RED in this panel are explicitly not launch approvals. Route them through AMK and qualified legal review before any public-facing claim or deploy.
external_reference_hint: EU AI Act, GDPR, Digital Services Act, and Pages deployment methods are summarized in companion docs — always verify primary sources with counsel.
